texto: We left the nursery in silence, Ma smoking a roll-up, looking like she hadn’t slept in a week or maybe like she had slept for a whole week. When the nursery was just a grubby finger smudge behind us Ma looked down at me and said in her poshed-up telephone voice: “Will you speak to Janie about swearing? Fuckin’ busybody!”I laughed and swung our linked hands.“Aye, fuckin’ busybody.”Pleas of laughter escaped us and spiraled up into the hot, blue Scottish sky. We laughed all the way home
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Kerry Hudson: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de temas ligados a humor. O trecho “We left the nursery in silence, Ma smoking a roll-up, looking like she hadn’t slept in a week or …” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que humor, drama, humour pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de reflexao-noturna e humour.

texto: Tony smothered the life that me and Ma had built, a furry mould growing over a sweating slab of cheese.
